title: If passed, AB 572 would mandate state contracts involving forest-risk commodities only go to companies with sustainable practices
prompt_preview:
[1 chunks] Task: Extract all distinct claims about AI or emerging technology in this press release. Do NOT assume a fixed number.
Output JSON only matching the provided schema.
Return concise, standalone claim_texts that could be tested against scientific literature. Exclude purely procedural statem
raw_preview:
```json
{
  "claims": [
    "AB 572 would mandate state contracts involving forest-risk commodities only go to companies with sustainable practices.",
    "Contractors doing business with the State of California would be required to have a No Deforestation, No Peat, No Exploitation (NDPE) policy.",
raw_text:
```json
{
  "claims": [
    "AB 572 would mandate state contracts involving forest-risk commodities only go to companies with sustainable practices.",
    "Contractors doing business with the State of California would be required to have a No Deforestation, No Peat, No Exploitation (NDPE) policy.",
    "By adopting a NDPE policy, contractors would ensure no deforestation of primary forests and areas of High Conservation Value.",
    "A NDPE policy would require a progressive reduction of emissions on existing plantations.",
    "The NDPE policy would respect Indigenous Peoples’ rights to give or withhold Free, Prior and Informed Consent for plantation operations on their customary lands.",
    "Tropical forests cover roughly seven percent of the Earth’s surface but harbor close to 50 percent of all species on Earth.",
    "An estimated 18 million acres of forest are lost every year due to human activities.",
    "Tropical deforestation is responsible for an estimated one-third of global CO2 emissions.",
    "California's purchasing power could be used to tackle the root causes of forest destruction."
  ]
}
```
